category_title: Trouble-makers who resist through sleeping, mobile phone use, and makeup
student_description: This student is highly defiant, occupies the back of the classroom to engage in off-task behaviors (sleeping, phone use, makeup), and treats aggressive pushback against teachers as an entertaining game. They uniquely require transactional, extrinsic rewards to engage in immediate tasks and highly pragmatic, exam-focused instruction to build long-term respect, while strictly avoiding formal disciplinary systems that trigger extreme escalation.

criteria.rationale: The student views non-exam-focused instruction as a waste of time and only respects teachers who explicitly highlight what will be tested.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term plan explicitly incorporates strategies to highlight crucial, exam-relevant points to build the student's perception of the tutor's value.

criteria.rationale: Forcing the student to move from the back of the classroom is a futile battle that the student will persistently subvert.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term plan avoids relying on forced seating arrangements or moving the student to the front of the class to manage behavior.
